payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

move.claim: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.
move.rationale: Standard ErdÅs-Rankin covering construction using prime powers for distinct primes; quantitative bounds from greedy covering or known tables tuned to match target growth.
move.test_plan: Implement small k=5,10 in Lean/Python: find explicit m_i, r_i covering [1,L]; scale to verify growth; if Lean times out, fallback to existence via combinatorial argument.
dependencies: Existence of covering systems with distinct prime power moduli
move.expected_effect: Provides the CRT covering for prime-free intervals of target length, advancing to parameter linking.
move.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches milestone 2; pairwise-coprime prime powers standard for CRT solvability without conflicts.
required_preconditions: Uncertain: exact constants c1,c2; assumes known covering densities
move.how_to_falsify_fast: For k=10, compute max L coverable with râ¤10 prime powers â¤ exp(10); if L < 10 log( exp(10) ) say, falsify growth.

payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\] formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

move.claim: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
move.rationale: Standard transfer: p_n â¤ N < p_{n+1}, so gap > L; PNT gives \log n â¼ \log N, matching log-scale terms asymptotically for large N.
move.test_plan: Symbolically verify inequality L > f(n) using \pi(N) â¼ N / \log N and change of variables; Lean tactic with real_asymp or logssimp.
move.expected_effect: Completes Milestone 1, reduces main goal to prime-free intervals.
move.why_this_applies_here: Direct analog to ErdÅs-Rankin reductions; log terms match problem exactly up to constants.
required_preconditions: PNT: |\pi(N) - li(N)| = o(N / \log N)
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Check if \log n = \log \pi(N) â¼ \log(N / \log N) = \log N + o(1) preserves the iterated log ratios; numeric eval for N=exp(exp(10)).

move.rationale: This is the core CRT covering lemma matching the subgoal formalization; construct via greedy prime powers covering residues, controlling exponents for log factors.
move.test_plan: Inductive construction: cover [1,L] with unions of arithmetic progressions mod small prime powers; bound r â¼ log L / log log L, verify Q growth.

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.

move.rationale: This is the canonical first reduction step in Erdős–Rankin style arguments.
move.test_plan: Check domain conditions for iterated logs and verify reduction inequalities symbolically.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
formalize: Construct a CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime moduli to force compositeness of a full interval block.

moves.claim: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.
moves.rationale: Standard ErdÅs-Rankin covering construction using prime powers for distinct primes; quantitative bounds from greedy covering or known tables tuned to match target growth.
moves.test_plan: Implement small k=5,10 in Lean/Python: find explicit m_i, r_i covering [1,L]; scale to verify growth; if Lean times out, fallback to existence via combinatorial argument.
dependencies: Existence of covering systems with distinct prime power moduli
moves.expected_effect: Provides the CRT covering for prime-free intervals of target length, advancing to parameter linking.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches milestone 2; pairwise-coprime prime powers standard for CRT solvability without conflicts.
required_preconditions: Uncertain: exact constants c1,c2; assumes known covering densities
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: For k=10, compute max L coverable with râ¤10 prime powers â¤ exp(10); if L < 10 log( exp(10) ) say, falsify growth.

moves.claim: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
moves.rationale: Standard transfer: p_n â¤ N < p_{n+1}, so gap > L; PNT gives \log n â¼ \log N, matching log-scale terms asymptotically for large N.
moves.test_plan: Symbolically verify inequality L > f(n) using \pi(N) â¼ N / \log N and change of variables; Lean tactic with real_asymp or logssimp.
moves.expected_effect: Completes Milestone 1, reduces main goal to prime-free intervals.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Direct analog to ErdÅs-Rankin reductions; log terms match problem exactly up to constants.
required_preconditions: PNT: |\pi(N) - li(N)| = o(N / \log N)
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: Check if \log n = \log \pi(N) â¼ \log(N / \log N) = \log N + o(1) preserves the iterated log ratios; numeric eval for N=exp(exp(10)).

moves.test_plan: Validate pairwise coprimality and non-collision constraints in a finite checker.
moves.expected_effect: Advances CRT milestone.

payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\] formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2. formalize: â c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, â distinct primes q1<...<qr â¤ k log k, ei â¥1, mi=qi^{ei} pairwise coprime, Q=â mi â¤ exp(c1 k), residues ri mod mi with â_i {d â [1,L] : d â¡ ri mod mi} = [1,L], L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2. formalize: â C > 0, if there exist infinitely many N â â with no primes in (N, N + L] and L > 2C  rac{\log N \log \log N \log \log \log \log N}{(\log \log \log N)^2}, then there exist infinitely many n with p_{n+1} - p_n > C  rac{\log n \log \log n \log \log \log \log n}{(\log \log \log n)^2} \log n, where for each such N we take n = Ï(N). formalize: Construct a CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime moduli to force compositeness of a full interval block. formalize: Construct a CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime moduli to force compositeness of a full interval block.

move.claim: â C > 0, if â infinitely many N â â with no primes in (N, N + L] and L > 2C (log N log log N log log log log N)/(log log log N)^2, then â infinitely many n with p_{n+1} - p_n > C (log n log log n log log log log n)/(log log log n)^2 log n, taking n = Ï(N) for each such N.
move.rationale: If (N, N+L] prime-free, then p_{Ï(N)+1} > N+L so gap at n=Ï(N) > L; asymptotic equivalence p_n â¼ n log n transfers log scales.
move.test_plan: Use PNT: li(N+L) - li(N) â¼ L/log N <1 implies no primes; verify log n â¼ log N - log log N preserves iterated log ratios symbolically in asymptotics.
dependencies: Prime Number Theorem: p_n â¼ n log n
move.expected_effect: Establishes reduction_interval_to_gap, linking interval gaps to consecutive prime gaps.
move.why_this_applies_here: Precisely matches Milestone 1 subgoal; prior attempt timed out but refined claim with explicit 2C factor and log matching avoids overflow.
required_preconditions: Iterated logs defined for N > exp(exp(exp(e)))
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Take known large gap N=10^18, L=10^6; compute target bound â¼10^5, check if gap > C * bound for C=1.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

move.claim: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
move.rationale: Standard reduction in prime gap proofs via PNT: gap exceeds L since p_{n+1} > N + L and p_n â¤ N, with li(N) ~ \pi(N) ~ n ensuring logarithmic scales match asymptotically.
move.test_plan: Verify symbolically: assume \pi(N) = li(N) + O(N / log^\omega N), derive p_n â¤ N < p_{n+1}, gap > L (1 - O(1/log N)) log N / li(N), check scaling.
move.expected_effect: Reduces main problem to infinitely many prime-free intervals of target length.
move.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches subgoal 'formalize...' for Milestone 1; refines failed attempt with explicit 2C factor and li(N) for faster Lean check.
required_preconditions: Asymptotic equivalence li(N) ~ \pi(N)
Monotonicity of primes
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Plug in N=exp(exp(10)), compute numerical L/li(N) log N vs target; fails if >10% discrepancy.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: â c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, â distinct primes q1<...<qr â¤ k log k, ei â¥1, mi=qi^{ei} pairwise coprime, Q=â mi â¤ exp(c1 k), residues ri mod mi with â_i {d â [1,L] : d â¡ ri mod mi} = [1,L], L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: â C > 0, if there exist infinitely many N â â with no primes in (N, N + L] and L > 2C  rac{\log N \log \log N \log \log \log \log N}{(\log \log \log N)^2}, then there exist infinitely many n with p_{n+1} - p_n > C  rac{\log n \log \log n \log \log \log \log n}{(\log \log \log n)^2} \log n, where for each such N we take n = Ï(N).

moves.claim: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.
moves.rationale: Standard ErdÅs-Rankin covering construction using prime powers for distinct primes; quantitative bounds from greedy covering or known tables tuned to match target growth.
moves.test_plan: Implement small k=5,10 in Lean/Python: find explicit m_i, r_i covering [1,L]; scale to verify growth; if Lean times out, fallback to existence via combinatorial argument.
dependencies: Existence of covering systems with distinct prime power moduli
moves.expected_effect: Provides the CRT covering for prime-free intervals of target length, advancing to parameter linking.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches milestone 2; pairwise-coprime prime powers standard for CRT solvability without conflicts.
required_preconditions: Uncertain: exact constants c1,c2; assumes known covering densities
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: For k=10, compute max L coverable with râ¤10 prime powers â¤ exp(10); if L < 10 log( exp(10) ) say, falsify growth.

moves.claim: â c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, â distinct primes q1<...<qr â¤ k log k, ei â¥1, mi=qi^{ei} pairwise coprime, Q=â mi â¤ exp(c1 k), residues ri mod mi with â_i {d â [1,L] : d â¡ ri mod mi} = [1,L], L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

moves.test_plan: Inductive construction: cover [1,L] with unions of arithmetic progressions mod small prime powers; bound r â¼ log L / log log L, verify Q growth.
moves.expected_effect: Proves formalize subgoal and crt_covering milestone.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Exact match to listed subgoal; transfers from classical sieve coverings (e.g., ErdÅs self-improving sieves) with iterated logs.
required_preconditions: Primes â¤ k log k suffice for small moduli density
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: Try small k=10: compute min Q covering L=100, check if L â³ (log Q loglog Q log4 Q)/(log3 Q)^2; if ratio <0.01, scale fails.

moves.claim: â C > 0, if there exist infinitely many N â â with no primes in (N, N + L] and L > 2C  rac{\log N \log \log N \log \log \log \log N}{(\log \log \log N)^2}, then there exist infinitely many n with p_{n+1} - p_n > C  rac{\log n \log \log n \log \log \log \log n}{(\log \log \log n)^2} \log n, where for each such N we take n = Ï(N).
moves.rationale: Standard transfer: p_n â¤ N < p_{n+1} with gap > L; PNT gives log n = log N + log(1/log N) + O(1) â¼ log N and iterated logs asymptotically equal, with factor 2 absorbing lower-order terms.
moves.test_plan: Formally prove using PNT Ï(N) â¼ N / log N: show lim inf (log n / log N) = 1, lim (log log n / log log N) = 1 etc.; verify L > 2C f(N) implies gap > C f(n) log n numerically for N=exp(exp(10)) and symbolically.
dependencies: Prime Number Theorem: Ï(N) â¼ N / log N
moves.expected_effect: Closes Milestone 1, reduces main goal to prime-free intervals.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Precisely matches 'Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound'; refines failed attempt with explicit constant 2C and asymptotic proof plan.
required_preconditions: PNT holds; infinitely many such N from later construction
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: Compute for N = exp(exp(5)): if f(n) log n / f(N) > 1/2 where n â¼ N/log N, reject; check in 1s numerically.

move.claim: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N+L] with N â¥ exp(exp(10)), L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then there are infinitely many n=Ï(N) with p_{n+1}-p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n, since |Ï(N)-li(N)| = O(N exp(-c \sqrt{\log N})) for c=1/10 implies Ï(N) â¼ li(N) â¼ N/\log N and the logs match up to constant factors.
move.rationale: Refined version of standard reduction with explicit error term and factor 2 to absorb approximations; avoids timeout by restricting to large N and using known explicit PNT bounds.
move.test_plan: Formally verify asymptotic equivalence log n = log(Ï(N)) = log N - log log N + O(1); numerically test for N=exp(1000) if L/2 > target gap function at n=li(N).
dependencies: |Ï(N) - li(N)| = O(N exp(-c sqrt(log N))) with c>0 explicit
move.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches multiple 'formalize:' subgoals on reduction_interval_to_gap; preconditions match via PNT.
required_preconditions: PNT error bound holds for N â¥ exp(exp(10))
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Compute for N=exp(100): if Ï(N+ L) = Ï(N), but target gap at Ï(N) < L/2, reject.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.

move.claim: \exists c_1, c_2 > 0 such that for arbitrarily large k, \exists distinct primes q_1 < \dots < q_r \le k \log k, e_i \ge 1, m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q = \prod m_i \le \exp(c_1 k), residues r_i \mod m_i with \bigcup_i \{d \in [1,L] : d \equiv r_i \mod m_i\} = [1,L], L \ge c_2 \frac{\log Q \log\log Q \log\log\log\log Q}{(\log\log\log Q)^2}.
move.rationale: Construct via greedy selection of prime powers: choose smallest primes q_i, optimize e_i = \lfloor \log L / \log q_i \rfloor +1 to balance coverage density L / \phi(m_i) \approx 1/r, ensuring full cover with r \sim k / \log k primes.
move.test_plan: Code implementation for k=20,30: enumerate primes <=k log k, greedily pick q_i^{e_i} maximizing new coverage per log m_i increment, verify union=[1,L], measure L/Q bound.
dependencies: Prime number theorem for pi(k log k) ~ k
move.expected_effect: Delivers explicit CRT system for prime-free intervals.
move.why_this_applies_here: Matches subgoal formalization exactly; pairwise coprime by distinct primes; scales to required L via iterated log optimization.
required_preconditions: Enough primes <=k log k (holds by PNT)
Uncertain: exact c1,c2; test small k
move.how_to_falsify_fast: For k=10, run greedy: if L < 0.01 * expression or Q > exp(0.1 k), falsify.

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
